rationale: The cited paper is referenced in core argument sections to support key premises that plan mismatch/overage and inertia are linked to churn in telecom settings. The citing authors explicitly use this prior evidence when selecting and motivating their pre-campaign behavioral variables (especially overage) for heterogeneity and churn analyses. Although the 2016 study does not reuse the 2007 Bayesian learning model, it relies on its empirical findings as part of the conceptual foundation for the field-experiment analysis.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Iyengar, Ansari, and Gupta (2007) as prior empirical grounding for churn drivers in access-service pricing, especially overage-related churn risk and inertia. This foundation helps motivate the citing paper's framing and variable design when testing churn effects of proactive plan recommendations.
